findings.evidence: `nl -ba code/TauCeti/Geometry/Diffeomorphism/FixingSubgroup.lean` shows the definition at lines 59-65 and the exported API through line 148 with no stabilizer theorem; `rg -n "fixingSubgroup_le_stabilizer" mathlib/Mathlib/GroupTheory/GroupAction/SubMulAction/OfFixingSubgroup.lean` gives the generic theorem at line 524.
findings.fix: Add a specialized wrapper, e.g. `theorem fixingSubgroup_le_stabilizer (s : Set M) : fixingSubgroup (I := I) (n := n) s ≤ MulAction.stabilizer (M ≃ₘ^n⟮I, I⟯ M) s`, proved directly or via a non-public import.
findings.issue: `Diffeomorph.fixingSubgroup` has no exported theorem that a pointwise fixer lies in the setwise stabilizer of the same subset, so users must unfold the specialization or import Mathlib's generic `MulAction.fixingSubgroup_le_stabilizer` themselves.

summary: The relative diffeomorphism fixer exposes the basic pointwise API, but it is still missing the characteristic setwise-stabilizer inclusion for the new specialized subgroup.
